concerns: Setting `spring.flyway.clean-on-validation-error=true` in `application.properties` is highly risky for production environments, as it can silently drop and recreate the database schema upon a validation mismatch, risking severe data loss.

The index `idx_notes_owner_created_at_desc` is defined on `(owner, created_at DESC)`. However, if the query pattern in the application sorts notes by `updated_at` first, the database will not utilize this index efficiently for sorting.

The contract uses camelCase names like `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, and `ownerId`, which might conflict if the backend is implemented in Python/SQLAlchemy or a database schema that prefers snake_case (`created_at`, `updated_at`, `owner_id`). This could require manual mapping layer/converters.

The unique note and owner identifiers are defined as UUID string format in OpenAPI, whereas some backend implementations use auto-incremented integer IDs (like in the Spring backend PR #0). Ensuring alignment on ID types across front-end/back-end is recommended.
